items.label: schema-dev
messages: Now let me explore the existing config, routes, and constants to understand what currently exists.

Now I have full context. Let me implement the schema-dev deliverables.  **Intent: The product-quick-view feature is a modal overlay on existing PLP pages. It requires NO new routes and NO config changes. My deliverable is shared constants (test IDs, modal configuration) that serve as a data contract between the storefront-dev (components/tests) and live-ui (E2E) agents.**
Now let me validate the constants file parses correctly and doesn't break the existing module interface:
Now let me verify the config file is still valid and check the routes file for any needed changes:
Good. Config is untouched and valid. Let me verify the final state of the constants file:
Now let me do a syntax check using the roam tool on the modified file:
Roam tools are blocked by the security policy. Falling back to standard tools. Let me verify the diff and commit:
Good, the diff shows exactly what I expect — 49 new lines added to `constants.js`. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

Schema-dev phase is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Schema-Dev Deliverables — product-quick-view  ### Changes Made **`overrides/app/constants.js`** — Added 3 shared constant objects as data contracts:  | Export | Purpose | Consumers | |---|---|---| | `QUICK_VIEW_TEST_IDS` | Centralised `data-testid` values (`quick-view-btn`, `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error`) | Product-tile override, QuickViewModal component, Jest unit tests, Playwright E2E selectors | | `QUICK_VIEW_CONFIG` | Modal configuration (`MODAL_SIZE: '4xl'`, `IMAGE_SIZE: 'sm'`, `SHOW_FULL_LINK: true`) | QuickViewModal component | | `QUICK_VIEW_OVERLAY` | Overlay bar style tokens (`BG`, `BG_ACTIVE`, `BACKDROP_FILTER`, `Z_INDEX`, `TRANSITION`) | Product-tile override, style assertions in tests |  ### What Was NOT Changed (by design) - **Routes** (`routes.jsx`): No new routes — Quick View is a modal overlay on existing PLP pages - **Config** (`config/default.js`): No API changes needed — feature uses existing `useProductViewModal` hook which relies on already-configured Shopper Products API - **Utils**: No shared utility functions needed — all logic is component-local  ### Notes - Roam MCP tools were blocked by security policy; standard tools were used for validation - Base constants re-export (`export * from '@salesforce/retail-react-app/app/constants'`) preserved to maintain module interface contract - Config validation passed: `node -e "require('./config/default')"` ✓

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && node -e " ↵ // Validate ESM constants file can be parsed (syntax check) ↵ const fs = require('fs'); ↵ const content = fs.readFileSync('./overrides/a

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && node -e ' ↵ const fs = require("fs"); ↵ const content = fs.readFileSync("./overrides/app/constants.js", "utf8"); ↵ console.log("File size:", content
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh all "feat(config): add Quick View shared constants data contract"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view schema-dev
